name: uidom
documentation:
# UiDOM ## An HTML library for python This library is inspired from dominate html library and takes it further. It supports jinja templating and many more features that we expect from an html library. We can even create Custom Elements and Web Components in UiDOM. ## Installation ```cmd > pip install uidom ``` ## An Alpinejs toggle example ```python #!/usr/bin/env python # app.py """ This example should work as is. """ from fastapi import FastAPI from uidom import Document from uidom.dom import Component, script, title, div from uidom.routing.fastapi import StreamingRoute document = Document(body=[ script(src="https://unpkg.com/alpinejs@3.10.2/dist/cdn.min.js", defer=None, rel="prefetch") ]) api = FastAPI() api.router.route_class = StreamingRoute class ToggleButton(Component): def render(self): with div(x_data={'open': 'true'}) as toggle: with div(x_on_click='open = !open'): div("Opened", x_show="open"), div("Closed", x_show="!open"), return toggle class App(Component): def render(self, *args, **kwargs): return document(*args, **kwargs, , head=title('App Page')) @api.get('/') def index(): return App(ToggleButton()) ``` ## A Jinja template example ```python from uidom.dom import nav, ul, For, li, a, Var, JinjaElement from collections import namedtuple as nt class Nav(JinjaElement): def render(self): return nav( ul( For( "item in menu_items", li(a(Var("item.name"), href=Var("item.link"))), ) ) ) # or we can write Jinja Element directly Nav = lambda: JinjaElement(nav(ul(For("item in menu_items", li(a(Var("item.name"), href=Var("item.link"))))))) nav_bar = Nav() menu_url = nt("menu_url", "name link") # nav_bar element is a jinja template and has an internal representation as follows ``` ```html
    {% for item in menu_items %}
  • {{ item.name }}
    • {% endfor %}
``` ```python # now we can use nav_bar just like we use jinja templates and render it as follows nav_bar( menu_items=[ menu_url("Home", r"\home.html"), menu_url("About", r"\about.html"), menu_url("Contact Us", r"\contact_us.html"), ] ) # it creates an element as follows ``` ```html
  • Home
  • About
  • Contact Us
``` ## using markdown with uidom elements ```python from uidom.dom import MarkdownElement em_text = MarkdownElement("*hello world*") print(em_text) # it returns following string ``` ```html

hello world

``` ```python # MarkdownElement can be used as follows too class HelloWorld(MarkdownElement): def render(self): return "*hello world*" # now HelloWorld instance gives the same # output print(HelloWorld()) ``` ```html

hello world

``` ## using raw html with uidom elements ```python from uidom.dom import * class Modal(Component): def render(self, *args, **kwargs): return '''

Confirm

''' ``` ## LICENSE ### this library is licensed under MIT (MIT-LICENSE)

methods.name: uidom.settings.commands.Command.run_command
documentation: command = valito.StringField(logger=False, debug=True)

classes.name: uidom.settings.commands.TailwindValidator
documentation: GROUPS = Union[str, list[str], None]
class GroupValidator(valio.Validator):
annotation = GROUPS
class GroupField(valio.Field):
validator = GroupValidator
@dataclass
class Group(Command):
groups = GroupField(logger=False, debug=True)
@groups.add_post_validator
def groups_as_list(self, group):
if isinstance(group, str):
return (group,)
return group
def exists(self) -> dict:
groups = dict()
for grp in self.group:
self.groups.logger.info(grp)
try:
result = self.run_command('getent', 'group', grp)
groups[grp] = result[1]
self.groups.logger.info(result)
except (Exception,) as e:
self.groups.logger.error(e)
groups[grp] = False
return groups
def add(self, name):
def remove(self, name):
group: GROUPS = groups.validator

class_variables.name: uidom.routing.fastapi.DirectoryRouter._METHODS
documentation:
DirectoryRouter class inherit from fastapi.APIRouter.

This class begins automatic route scanning upon initialization.

Args:
    base_directory (str, optional): Defines the folder to be subjected to automatic route scanning.
    route_file_name (str, optional): Specifies the file name within the folder where route scanning will be conducted

Examples:
    >>> from fastapi import FastAPI
    >>> from uidom.routing.fastapi import DirectoryRouter
    >>> app = FastAPI()
    >>> dir_router = DirectoryRouter()
    >>> app.include_router(prefix="/dir-routes", router=dir_router)

Todo:
    * For each route configuration, the __config__ variable will be searched within the files.
    * Error and exception handling scenarios will be enhanced/developed.

methods.name: uidom.web_io._adapter.WebSocketAdapter.on_connect
documentation:
Callback function called on WebSocket connection.

Args:
    websocket (WebSocket): The WebSocket instance.

methods.name: uidom.web_io._adapter.WebSocketAdapter.on_disconnect
documentation:
Callback function called on WebSocket disconnection.

Args:
    websocket (WebSocket): The WebSocket instance.

methods.name: uidom.web_io._adapter.WebSocketAdapter.on_receive
documentation:
Callback function called on receiving a message over the WebSocket.

Args:
    websocket (WebSocket): The WebSocket instance.
    data (dict): The message data, with an "event" key indicating the event name and any additional parameters.

A WebSocket adapter that handles stateful communication with a data class instance.

Attributes:
    class_def (type): The class of the data object to be instantiated.
    events (dict): A mapping of event names to the corresponding method names in the data class.
    data_fetcher (DataFetcher, optional): A data fetcher object to retrieve data from a data store.
                                           Defaults to None, in which case a new instance of the data_class is used.
    class_instance (Any): The instance of the class_def to be used in stateful communication.

methods.name: uidom.web_io._adapter.EdgeDBFetcher.fetch

Args:

    **kwargs: Additional keyword arguments.

Returns:
    Any: An instance of the specified data class.

A data fetcher that retrieves data from an EdgeDB database.

Attributes:
    dsn (str): The DSN of the EdgeDB database to connect to.

modules.name: uidom.dom.src.svgtags
documentation:
This module consists of classes specific to HTML5-SVG Elements. In general this module does not include
- Elements that are not specific to SVG (eg. <a>)
- Elements that are deprecated
Copyright (c) 2022 uidom
This software is released under the MIT License.
https://opensource.org/licenses/MIT

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.svgtags.svg_tag
documentation: https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/SVG/Element/svg

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.svgtags.animate
documentation:
The  animate SVG element is used to animate an attribute or property of an element over time.
It's normally inserted inside the element or referenced by the href attribute of the target element.

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.svgtags.animateMotion
documentation: The <animateMotion> element causes a referenced element to move along a motion path.

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.svgtags.animateTransform
documentation:
The animateTransform element animates a transformation attribute on its target element, thereby allowing
animations to control translation, scaling, rotation, and/or skewing.

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.svgtags.circle
documentation: The <circle> SVG element is an SVG basic shape, used to draw circles based on a center point and a radius.

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.svgtags.clipPath
documentation: The <clipPath> SVG element defines a clipping path, to be used used by the clip-path property.

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.svgtags.defs
documentation:
The <defs> element is used to store graphical objects that will be used at a later time. Objects created inside a
<defs> element are not rendered directly. To display them you have to reference them
(with a <use> element for example).

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.svgtags.desc
documentation: The <desc> element provides an accessible, long-text description of any SVG container element or graphics element.

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.svgtags.ellipse
documentation: An ellipse element for svg containers

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.svgtags.g
documentation: The <g> SVG element is a container used to group other SVG elements.
(Note, filters are at the bottom of this file)

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.svgtags.image
documentation: The <image> SVG element includes images inside SVG documents. It can display raster image files or other SVG files.

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.svgtags.line
documentation: The <line> element is an SVG basic shape used to create a line connecting two points.

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.svgtags.linearGradient
documentation:
The <linearGradient> element lets authors define linear gradients that can be applied to fill or
stroke of graphical elements.

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.svgtags.marker
documentation: The <marker> element defines the graphic that is to be used for drawing arrowheads or polymarkers on a given <path>, <line>, <polyline> or <polygon> element.

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.svgtags.mask
documentation:
The <mask> element defines an alpha mask for compositing the current object into the background.
A mask is used/referenced using the mask property.

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.svgtags.mpath
documentation:
The <mpath> sub-element for the <animateMotion> element provides the ability to reference an
external <path> element as the definition of a motion path.

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.svgtags.pattern
documentation:
The <pattern> element defines a graphics object which can be redrawn at repeated x and y-coordinate
intervals ("tiled") to cover an area.

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.svgtags.polygon
documentation: A polygon element for svg containers

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.svgtags.polyline
documentation: A polyline element for svg containers

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.svgtags.radialGradient
documentation:
The <radialGradient> element lets authors define radial gradients that can be applied to fill
or stroke of graphical elements.

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.svgtags.path
documentation: A path element for svg containers

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.svgtags.rect
documentation: A rectangle element for svg containers

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.svgtags.stop
documentation:
The SVG <stop> element defines a color and its position to use on a gradient.
This element is always a child of a <linearGradient> or <radialGradient> element.

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.svgtags.switch
documentation:
The <switch> SVG element evaluates any requiredFeatures, requiredExtensions and systemLanguage attributes
on its direct child elements in order, and then renders the first child where these attributes evaluate to true.
Other direct children will be bypassed and therefore not rendered. If a child element is a container element,
like <g>, then its subtree is also processed/rendered or bypassed/not rendered.

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.svgtags.symbol
documentation:
The use of symbol elements for graphics that are used multiple times in the same document adds structure and
 semantics. Documents that are rich in structure may be rendered graphically, as speech, or as Braille,
 and thus promote accessibility.

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.svgtags.text
documentation:
The SVG <text> element draws a graphics element consisting of text. It's possible to apply a gradient,
 pattern, clipping path, mask, or filter to <text>, like any other SVG graphics element.

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.svgtags.textPath
documentation:
To render text along the shape of a <path>, enclose the text in a <textPath> element that has an href
attribute with a reference to the <path> element.

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.svgtags.title
documentation:
The <title> element provides an accessible, short-text description of any SVG container
element or graphics element.

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.svgtags.tspan
documentation:
The SVG <tspan> element define a subtext within a <text> element or another <tspan> element.
It allows to adjust the style and/or position of that subtext as needed.

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.svgtags.use
documentation: The <use> element takes nodes from within the SVG document, and duplicates them somewhere else.

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.svgtags.view
documentation: A view is a defined way to view the image, like a zoom level or a detail view.

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.svgtags.filter
documentation: FILTERS

https://opensource.org/licenses/MIT

functions.name: uidom.dom.src.html_string.defHTML
documentation: don't decorate defHTML with functools.lru_cache because if defHTML is used in isolation
like:
with div() as parent_div:
child_element = defHTML("some html string")
with lru_cache decorated on defHTML the evaluation of child_element at run time will not happen twice
and thus parent_div will never add child_element in subsequest runs thus better way to cache defHTML
evaluation is to cache the method where it is used.

methods.name: uidom.dom.src.htmltags.html_tag.__init__
documentation: Creates a new html tag instance.

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.htmltags.html
documentation: The html element represents the root of an HTML document.
Root element

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.htmltags.head
documentation: The head element represents a collection of metadata for the document.
Document metadata

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.htmltags.title
documentation:
The title element represents the document's title or name. Authors should use
titles that identify their documents even when they are used out of context,
for example in a user's history or bookmarks, or in search results. The
document's title is often different from its first heading, since the first
heading does not have to stand alone when taken out of context.

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.htmltags.base
documentation:
The base element allows authors to specify the document base URL for the
purposes of resolving relative URLs, and the name of the default browsing
context for the purposes of following hyperlinks. The element does not
represent any content beyond this information.

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.htmltags.link
documentation: The link element allows authors to link their document to other resources.

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.htmltags.meta
documentation:
The meta element represents various kinds of metadata that cannot be
expressed using the title, base, link, style, and script elements.

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.htmltags.style
documentation:
The style element allows authors to embed style information in their
documents. The style element is one of several inputs to the styling
processing model. The element does not represent content for the user.

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.htmltags.script
documentation:
The script element allows authors to include dynamic script and data blocks
in their documents. The element does not represent content for the user.
Scripting

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.htmltags.noscript
documentation:
The noscript element represents nothing if scripting is enabled, and
represents its children if scripting is disabled. It is used to present
different markup to user agents that support scripting and those that don't
support scripting, by affecting how the document is parsed.

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.htmltags.body
documentation: The body element represents the main content of the document.
Sections

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.htmltags.main
documentation:
The main content area of a document includes content that is unique to that
document and excludes content that is repeated across a set of documents such
as site navigation links, copyright information, site logos and banners and
search forms (unless the document or application's main function is that of a
search form).

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.htmltags.section
documentation:
The section element represents a generic section of a document or
application. A section, in this context, is a thematic grouping of content,
typically with a heading.

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.htmltags.nav
documentation:
The nav element represents a section of a page that links to other pages or
to parts within the page: a section with navigation links.

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.htmltags.article
documentation:
The article element represents a self-contained composition in a document,
page, application, or site and that is, in principle, independently
distributable or reusable, e.g. in syndication. This could be a forum post, a
magazine or newspaper article, a blog entry, a user-submitted comment, an
interactive widget or gadget, or any other independent item of content.

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.htmltags.aside
documentation:
The aside element represents a section of a page that consists of content
that is tangentially related to the content around the aside element, and
which could be considered separate from that content. Such sections are
often represented as sidebars in printed typography.

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.htmltags.h1
documentation: Represents the highest ranking heading.

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.htmltags.h2
documentation: Represents the second-highest ranking heading.

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.htmltags.h3
documentation: Represents the third-highest ranking heading.

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.htmltags.h4
documentation: Represents the fourth-highest ranking heading.

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.htmltags.h5
documentation: Represents the fifth-highest ranking heading.

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.htmltags.h6
documentation: Represents the sixth-highest ranking heading.

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.htmltags.hgroup
documentation:
The hgroup element represents the heading of a section. The element is used
to group a set of h1-h6 elements when the heading has multiple levels, such
as subheadings, alternative titles, or taglines.

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.htmltags.header
documentation: The header element represents a group of introductory or navigational aids.

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.htmltags.footer
documentation:
The footer element represents a footer for its nearest ancestor sectioning
content or sectioning root element. A footer typically contains information
about its section such as who wrote it, links to related documents,
copyright data, and the like.

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.htmltags.address
documentation:
The address element represents the contact information for its nearest
article or body element ancestor. If that is the body element, then the
contact information applies to the document as a whole.

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.htmltags.p
documentation: The p element represents a paragraph.
Grouping content

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.htmltags.hr
documentation:
The hr element represents a paragraph-level thematic break, e.g. a scene
change in a story, or a transition to another topic within a section of a
reference book.

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.htmltags.pre
documentation:
The pre element represents a block of preformatted text, in which structure
is represented by typographic conventions rather than by elements.

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.htmltags.blockquote
documentation:
The blockquote element represents a section that is quoted from another
source.

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.htmltags.ol
documentation:
The ol element represents a list of items, where the items have been
intentionally ordered, such that changing the order would change the
meaning of the document.

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.htmltags.ul
documentation:
The ul element represents a list of items, where the order of the items is
not important - that is, where changing the order would not materially change
the meaning of the document.

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.htmltags.li
documentation:
The li element represents a list item. If its parent element is an ol, ul, or
menu element, then the element is an item of the parent element's list, as
defined for those elements. Otherwise, the list item has no defined
list-related relationship to any other li element.

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.htmltags.dl
documentation:
The dl element represents an association list consisting of zero or more
name-value groups (a description list). Each group must consist of one or
more names (dt elements) followed by one or more values (dd elements).
Within a single dl element, there should not be more than one dt element for
each name.

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.htmltags.dt
documentation:
The dt element represents the term, or name, part of a term-description group
in a description list (dl element).

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.htmltags.dd
documentation:
The dd element represents the description, definition, or value, part of a
term-description group in a description list (dl element).

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.htmltags.figure
documentation:
The figure element represents some flow content, optionally with a caption,
that is self-contained and is typically referenced as a single unit from the
main flow of the document.

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.htmltags.figcaption
documentation:
The figcaption element represents a caption or legend for the rest of the
contents of the figcaption element's parent figure element, if any.

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.htmltags.div
documentation:
The div element has no special meaning at all. It represents its children. It
can be used with the class, lang, and title attributes to mark up semantics
common to a group of consecutive elements.

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.htmltags.a
documentation:
If the a element has an href attribute, then it represents a hyperlink (a
hypertext anchor).

If the a element has no href attribute, then the element represents a
placeholder for where a link might otherwise have been placed, if it had been
relevant.
Text semantics

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.htmltags.em
documentation: The em element represents stress emphasis of its contents.

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.htmltags.strong
documentation: The strong element represents strong importance for its contents.

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.htmltags.small
documentation: The small element represents side comments such as small print.

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.htmltags.s
documentation:
The s element represents contents that are no longer accurate or no longer
relevant.

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.htmltags.cite
documentation:
The cite element represents the title of a work (e.g. a book, a paper, an
essay, a poem, a score, a song, a script, a film, a TV show, a game, a
sculpture, a painting, a theatre production, a play, an opera, a musical, an
exhibition, a legal case report, etc). This can be a work that is being
quoted or referenced in detail (i.e. a citation), or it can just be a work
that is mentioned in passing.

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.htmltags.q
documentation: The q element represents some phrasing content quoted from another source.

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.htmltags.dfn
documentation:
The dfn element represents the defining instance of a term. The paragraph,
description list group, or section that is the nearest ancestor of the dfn
element must also contain the definition(s) for the term given by the dfn
element.

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.htmltags.abbr
documentation:
The abbr element represents an abbreviation or acronym, optionally with its
expansion. The title attribute may be used to provide an expansion of the
abbreviation. The attribute, if specified, must contain an expansion of the
abbreviation, and nothing else.

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.htmltags.time_
documentation:
The time element represents either a time on a 24 hour clock, or a precise
date in the proleptic Gregorian calendar, optionally with a time and a
time-zone offset.

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.htmltags.code
documentation:
The code element represents a fragment of computer code. This could be an XML
element name, a filename, a computer program, or any other string that a
computer would recognize.

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.htmltags.var
documentation:
The var element represents a variable. This could be an actual variable in a
mathematical expression or programming context, an identifier representing a
constant, a function parameter, or just be a term used as a placeholder in
prose.

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.htmltags.samp
documentation:
The samp element represents (sample) output from a program or computing
system.

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.htmltags.kbd
documentation:
The kbd element represents user input (typically keyboard input, although it
may also be used to represent other input, such as voice commands).

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.htmltags.sub
documentation: The sub element represents a subscript.

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.htmltags.sup
documentation: The sup element represents a superscript.

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.htmltags.i
documentation:
The i element represents a span of text in an alternate voice or mood, or
otherwise offset from the normal prose in a manner indicating a different
quality of text, such as a taxonomic designation, a technical term, an
idiomatic phrase from another language, a thought, or a ship name in Western
texts.

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.htmltags.b
documentation:
The b element represents a span of text to which attention is being drawn for
utilitarian purposes without conveying any extra importance and with no
implication of an alternate voice or mood, such as key words in a document
abstract, product names in a review, actionable words in interactive
text-driven software, or an article lede.

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.htmltags.u
documentation:
The u element represents a span of text with an unarticulated, though
explicitly rendered, non-textual annotation, such as labeling the text as
being a proper name in Chinese text (a Chinese proper name mark), or
labeling the text as being misspelt.

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.htmltags.mark
documentation:
The mark element represents a run of text in one document marked or
highlighted for reference purposes, due to its relevance in another context.
When used in a quotation or other block of text referred to from the prose,
it indicates a highlight that was not originally present but which has been
added to bring the reader's attention to a part of the text that might not
have been considered important by the original author when the block was
originally written, but which is now under previously unexpected scrutiny.
When used in the main prose of a document, it indicates a part of the
document that has been highlighted due to its likely relevance to the user's
current activity.

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.htmltags.ruby
documentation:
The ruby element allows one or more spans of phrasing content to be marked
with ruby annotations. Ruby annotations are short runs of text presented
alongside base text, primarily used in East Asian typography as a guide for
pronunciation or to include other annotations. In Japanese, this form of
typography is also known as furigana.

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.htmltags.rt
documentation: The rt element marks the ruby text component of a ruby annotation.

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.htmltags.rp
documentation:
The rp element can be used to provide parentheses around a ruby text
component of a ruby annotation, to be shown by user agents that don't support
ruby annotations.

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.htmltags.bdi
documentation:
The bdi element represents a span of text that is to be isolated from its
surroundings for the purposes of bidirectional text formatting.

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.htmltags.bdo
documentation:
The bdo element represents explicit text directionality formatting control
for its children. It allows authors to override the Unicode bidirectional
algorithm by explicitly specifying a direction override.

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.htmltags.span
documentation:
The span element doesn't mean anything on its own, but can be useful when
used together with the global attributes, e.g. class, lang, or dir. It
represents its children.

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.htmltags.br
documentation: The br element represents a line break.

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.htmltags.wbr
documentation: The wbr element represents a line break opportunity.

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.htmltags.ins
documentation: The ins element represents an addition to the document.
Edits

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.htmltags.del_
documentation: The del element represents a removal from the document.

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.htmltags.img
documentation: An img element represents an image.
Embedded content

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.htmltags.iframe
documentation: The iframe element represents a nested browsing context.

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.htmltags.embed
documentation:
The embed element represents an integration point for an external (typically
non-HTML) application or interactive content.

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.htmltags.object_
documentation:
The object element can represent an external resource, which, depending on
the type of the resource, will either be treated as an image, as a nested
browsing context, or as an external resource to be processed by a plugin.

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.htmltags.param
documentation:
The param element defines parameters for plugins invoked by object elements.
It does not represent anything on its own.

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.htmltags.video
documentation:
A video element is used for playing videos or movies, and audio files with
captions.

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.htmltags.audio
documentation: An audio element represents a sound or audio stream.

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.htmltags.source
documentation:
The source element allows authors to specify multiple alternative media
resources for media elements. It does not represent anything on its own.

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.htmltags.track
documentation:
The track element allows authors to specify explicit external timed text
tracks for media elements. It does not represent anything on its own.

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.htmltags.canvas
documentation:
The canvas element provides scripts with a resolution-dependent bitmap
canvas, which can be used for rendering graphs, game graphics, or other
visual images on the fly.

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.htmltags.map_
documentation:
The map element, in conjunction with any area element descendants, defines an
image map. The element represents its children.

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.htmltags.area
documentation:
The area element represents either a hyperlink with some text and a
corresponding area on an image map, or a dead area on an image map.

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.htmltags.table
documentation:
The table element represents data with more than one dimension, in the form
of a table.
Tabular data

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.htmltags.caption
documentation:
The caption element represents the title of the table that is its parent, if
it has a parent and that is a table element.

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.htmltags.colgroup
documentation:
The colgroup element represents a group of one or more columns in the table
that is its parent, if it has a parent and that is a table element.

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.htmltags.col
documentation:
If a col element has a parent and that is a colgroup element that itself has
a parent that is a table element, then the col element represents one or more
columns in the column group represented by that colgroup.

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.htmltags.tbody
documentation:
The tbody element represents a block of rows that consist of a body of data
for the parent table element, if the tbody element has a parent and it is a
table.

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.htmltags.thead
documentation:
The thead element represents the block of rows that consist of the column
labels (headers) for the parent table element, if the thead element has a
parent and it is a table.

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.htmltags.tfoot
documentation:
The tfoot element represents the block of rows that consist of the column
summaries (footers) for the parent table element, if the tfoot element has a
parent and it is a table.

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.htmltags.tr
documentation: The tr element represents a row of cells in a table.

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.htmltags.td
documentation: The td element represents a data cell in a table.

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.htmltags.th
documentation: The th element represents a header cell in a table.

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.htmltags.form
documentation:
The form element represents a collection of form-associated elements, some of
which can represent editable values that can be submitted to a server for
processing.
Forms

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.htmltags.fieldset
documentation:
The fieldset element represents a set of form controls optionally grouped
under a common name.

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.htmltags.legend
documentation:
The legend element represents a caption for the rest of the contents of the
legend element's parent fieldset element, if any.

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.htmltags.label
documentation:
The label represents a caption in a user interface. The caption can be
associated with a specific form control, known as the label element's labeled
control, either using for attribute, or by putting the form control inside
the label element itself.

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.htmltags.input_
documentation:
The input element represents a typed data field, usually with a form control
to allow the user to edit the data.

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.htmltags.button
documentation:
The button element represents a button. If the element is not disabled, then
the user agent should allow the user to activate the button.

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.htmltags.select
documentation:
The select element represents a control for selecting amongst a set of
options.

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.htmltags.datalist
documentation:
The datalist element represents a set of option elements that represent
predefined options for other controls. The contents of the element represents
fallback content for legacy user agents, intermixed with option elements that
represent the predefined options. In the rendering, the datalist element
represents nothing and it, along with its children, should be hidden.

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.htmltags.optgroup
documentation:
The optgroup element represents a group of option elements with a common
label.

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.htmltags.option
documentation:
The option element represents an option in a select element or as part of a
list of suggestions in a datalist element.

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.htmltags.textarea
documentation:
The textarea element represents a multiline plain text edit control for the
element's raw value. The contents of the control represent the control's
default value.

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.htmltags.keygen
documentation:
The keygen element represents a key pair generator control. When the
control's form is submitted, the private key is stored in the local keystore,
and the public key is packaged and sent to the server.

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.htmltags.output
documentation: The output element represents the result of a calculation.

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.htmltags.progress
documentation:
The progress element represents the completion progress of a task. The
progress is either indeterminate, indicating that progress is being made but
that it is not clear how much more work remains to be done before the task is
complete (e.g. because the task is waiting for a remote host to respond), or
the progress is a number in the range zero to a maximum, giving the fraction
of work that has so far been completed.

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.htmltags.meter
documentation:
The meter element represents a scalar measurement within a known range, or a
fractional value; for example disk usage, the relevance of a query result, or
the fraction of a voting population to have selected a particular candidate.

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.htmltags.details
documentation:
The details element represents a disclosure widget from which the user can
obtain additional information or controls.
Interactive elements

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.htmltags.summary
documentation:
The summary element represents a summary, caption, or legend for the rest of
the contents of the summary element's parent details element, if any.

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.htmltags.command
documentation: The command element represents a command that the user can invoke.

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.htmltags.menu
documentation: The menu element represents a list of commands.

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.htmltags.font
documentation: The font element represents the font in a html .

class_variables.name: uidom.dom.src.htmltags.comment.enable_right_delimiter_space
documentation: <-- comments -->

methods.name: uidom.dom.src.component.Component.add
documentation: Adding tags to a component appends them to the render.

methods.name: uidom.dom.src.component.Component.call
documentation:
This is basically a placeholder for using websocket communications.
All sorts of fun stuffs can happens here.
\:param args:
\:param kwargs:
\:return:

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.component.Fragment
documentation:
Its just a placeholder which renders all its childrens
    but not itself and passes all it attributes to its childrens.

Args:
    None

Returns:
    Fragment: it returns fragment which renders all its childrens

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.component.MergeClassAttribute
documentation:
Merging the attributes with subcontexts via uidom.dom.src.dom_tag.attr

Args:
    None:
Usage:
    with MergeClassAttribute():
        attr(className=...)
        attr(className=...)
        div()
        ### this div receives all the attributes set via attr methods contexts
        ### but all the className kwargs are merged..

This software is released under the MIT License.
https://opensource.org/licenses/MIT

functions.name: uidom.dom.src.dom_tag.get_current
documentation:
get the current tag being used as a with context or decorated function.
if no context is active, raises ValueError, or returns the default, if provided

functions.name: uidom.dom.src.dom_tag.attr
documentation: Set attributes on the current active tag context

variables.name: uidom.dom.src.dom_tag.basestring
documentation: type: ignore

methods.name: uidom.dom.src.dom_tag.dom_tag.__new__
documentation:
Check if bare tag is being used as a decorator
(called with a single function arg).
decorate the function and return

methods.name: uidom.dom.src.dom_tag.dom_tag.__init__
documentation:
Creates a new tag. Child tags should be passed as arguments and attributes
should be passed as keyword arguments.

There is a non-rendering attribute which controls how the tag renders:

* `__inline` - Boolean value. If True renders all children tags on the same
               line.

methods.name: uidom.dom.src.dom_tag.dom_tag.__call__
documentation:
tag instance is being used as a decorator.
wrap func to make a copy of this tag

methods.name: uidom.dom.src.dom_tag.dom_tag.set_attribute
documentation: Add or update the value of an attribute.

methods.name: uidom.dom.src.dom_tag.dom_tag.setdocument
documentation:
Creates a reference to the parent document to allow for partial-tree
validation.

methods.name: uidom.dom.src.dom_tag.dom_tag.add
documentation: Add new child tags.

methods.name: uidom.dom.src.dom_tag.dom_tag.get
documentation:
Recursively searches children for tags of a certain
type with matching attributes.

methods.name: uidom.dom.src.dom_tag.dom_tag.__getitem__
documentation:
Returns the stored value of the specified attribute or child
(if it exists).

methods.name: uidom.dom.src.dom_tag.dom_tag.__len__
documentation: Number of child elements.

methods.name: uidom.dom.src.dom_tag.dom_tag.__bool__
documentation: Hack for "if x" and __len__

methods.name: uidom.dom.src.dom_tag.dom_tag.__iter__
documentation: Iterates over child elements.

methods.name: uidom.dom.src.dom_tag.dom_tag.__contains__
documentation:
Checks recursively if item is in children tree.
Accepts both a string and a class.

methods.name: uidom.dom.src.dom_tag.dom_tag.__iadd__
documentation: Reflexive binary addition simply adds tag as a child.

methods.name: uidom.dom.src.dom_tag.dom_tag.__unicode__
documentation: String and unicode representations are the same as __render__()

methods.name: uidom.dom.src.dom_tag.dom_tag.clean_attribute
documentation:
Normalize attribute names for shorthand and work arounds for limitations
in Python's syntax

methods.name: uidom.dom.src.dom_tag.dom_tag.clean_pair
documentation:
This will call `clean_attribute` on the attribute and also allows for the
creation of boolean attributes.

Ex. input(selected=True) is equivalent to input(selected="selected")

class_variables.name: uidom.dom.src.dom_tag.dom_tag.is_single
documentation: Tag does not require matching end tag (ex. <hr/>)

class_variables.name: uidom.dom.src.dom_tag.dom_tag.is_pretty
documentation: Text inside the tag should be left as-is (ex. <pre>)

modules.name: uidom.dom.src.utils.sheets
documentation: Copyright (c) 2022 uidom
This software is released under the MIT License.
https://opensource.org/licenses/MIT

modules.name: uidom.dom.src.parse_html
documentation:
A simple but complete HTML to Abstract Syntax Tree (AST) parser. The AST can also reproduce the HTML text. Example: ``` >> text = '

text

' >> ast = tokenize_html(text) >> list(ast.walk(include_self=True)) [Root(''), Tag('div', {'class': 'note'}), Tag('p'), Data('text')] >> str(ast) '

text

' >> str(ast[0][0]) '

text

' ``` Note: optional tags are not accounted for (see )

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.parse_html.Attribute

methods.name: uidom.dom.src.parse_html.Attribute.__getitem__
documentation: If self doesn't have the key it returns ''.

methods.name: uidom.dom.src.parse_html.Attribute.classes
documentation: Return 'class' attribute as list.

methods.name: uidom.dom.src.parse_html.Attribute.__str__
documentation: Return a htmlized representation for attributes.
This class holds the tags's attributes.

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.parse_html.Element

methods.name: uidom.dom.src.parse_html.Element.__init__
documentation: Initialise the element.

methods.name: uidom.dom.src.parse_html.Element.parent
documentation: Return parent.

methods.name: uidom.dom.src.parse_html.Element.children
documentation: Return copy of children.

methods.name: uidom.dom.src.parse_html.Element.deepcopy
documentation: Recursively copy and remove parent.

methods.name: uidom.dom.src.parse_html.Element.render
documentation:
Returns a HTML string representation of the element.

```
param tag_overrides

Provide a dictionary of render function
for specific tag names, to override the normal render format
```

methods.name: uidom.dom.src.parse_html.Element.walk
documentation: Walk through the xml/html AST.

methods.name: uidom.dom.src.parse_html.Element.strip
documentation:
Return copy with all `Data` tokens
that only contain whitespace / newlines removed.

methods.name: uidom.dom.src.parse_html.Element.find
documentation: Find all elements that match name and specific attributes.

An Element of the xml/html document.

All xml/html entities inherit from this class.

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.parse_html.Root

methods.name: uidom.dom.src.parse_html.Root.render
documentation: Returns a string HTML representation of the structure.
The root of the AST tree.

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.parse_html.Tag
documentation: Represent xml/html tags under the form: <name key="value" ...> ... </name>.

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.parse_html.XTag
documentation: Represent XHTML style tags with no children, like ``

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.parse_html.VoidTag
documentation: Represent tags with no children, only start tag, like ``

methods.name: uidom.dom.src.parse_html.TerminalElement.deepcopy
documentation: Copy and remove parent.

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.parse_html.Data
documentation: Represent data inside xml/html documents, like raw text.

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.parse_html.Declaration
documentation: Represent declarations, like `<!DOCTYPE html>`

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.parse_html.Comment
documentation: Represent HTML comments

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.parse_html.Pi
documentation: Represent processing instructions like `<?xml-stylesheet ?>`

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.parse_html.Char
documentation: Represent character codes like: `&#0`

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.parse_html.Entity
documentation: Represent entities like `&amp`

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.parse_html.Tree

methods.name: uidom.dom.src.parse_html.Tree.__init__
documentation: Initialise Tree

methods.name: uidom.dom.src.parse_html.Tree.clear
documentation: Clear the outmost and stack for a new parsing.

methods.name: uidom.dom.src.parse_html.Tree.last
documentation: Return the last pointer which point to the actual tag scope.

methods.name: uidom.dom.src.parse_html.Tree.nest_tag
documentation:
Nest a given tag at the bottom of the tree using
the last stack's pointer.

methods.name: uidom.dom.src.parse_html.Tree.nest_xtag
documentation: Nest an XTag onto the tree.

methods.name: uidom.dom.src.parse_html.Tree.nest_vtag
documentation: Nest a VoidTag onto the tree.

methods.name: uidom.dom.src.parse_html.Tree.nest_terminal
documentation: Nest the data onto the tree.

methods.name: uidom.dom.src.parse_html.Tree.enclose
documentation:
When a closing tag is found, pop the pointer's scope from the stack,
to then point to the earlier scope's tag.
The engine class to generate the AST tree.

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.parse_html.HtmlToAst

methods.name: uidom.dom.src.parse_html.HtmlToAst.feed
documentation: Parse the source string.

methods.name: uidom.dom.src.parse_html.HtmlToAst.handle_starttag
documentation: When found an opening tag then nest it onto the tree.

methods.name: uidom.dom.src.parse_html.HtmlToAst.handle_startendtag
documentation: When found a XHTML tag style then nest it up to the tree.

methods.name: uidom.dom.src.parse_html.HtmlToAst.handle_endtag
documentation: When found a closing tag then makes it point to the right scope.

methods.name: uidom.dom.src.parse_html.HtmlToAst.handle_data
documentation: Nest data onto the tree.
The tokenizer class.

modules.name: uidom.dom.src.utils.dom_util

functions.name: uidom.dom.src.utils.dom_util.system
documentation: pipes the output of a program

functions.name: uidom.dom.src.utils.dom_util.escape
documentation:
Escapes special characters into their html entities
Replace special characters "&", "<" and ">" to HTML-safe sequences.
If the optional flag quote is true, the quotation mark character (")
is also translated.

This is used to escape content that appears in the body of an HTML document

functions.name: uidom.dom.src.utils.dom_util.unescape
documentation: unescapes html entities. the opposite of escape.

functions.name: uidom.dom.src.utils.dom_util.raw
documentation: Inserts a raw string into the DOM. Unsafe.

classes.name: uidom.dom.src.utils.dom_util.lazy

methods.name: uidom.dom.src.utils.dom_util.lazy.__new__
documentation:
Need to reset this special method or else
dom_tag will think it's being used as a dectorator.

This means lazy() can't be used as a dectorator, but
thinking about when you might want that just confuses me.
delays function execution until rendered

modules.name: uidom.dom.uniqueid
documentation: Copyright (c) 2023 UiDOM
This software is released under the MIT License.
https://opensource.org/licenses/MIT

functions.name: uidom.dom.uniqueid._uniqueid
documentation:
generate unique id with length 17 to 21.
ensure uniqueness even with daylight savings events (clocks adjusted one-hour backward).

if you generate 1 million ids per second during 100 years, you will generate
2\*25 (approx sec per year) * 10\*\*6 (1 million id per sec) * 100 (years) = 5 * 10\*\*9 unique ids.

with 17 digits (radix 16) id, you can represent 16\*\*17 = 295147905179352825856 ids (around 2.9 * 10\*\*20).
In fact, as we need far less than that, we agree that the format used to represent id (seed + timestamp reversed)
do not cover all numbers that could be represented with 35 digits (radix 16).

if you generate 1 million id per second with this algorithm, it will increase the seed by less than 2\*\*12 per hour
so if a DST occurs and backward one hour, we need to ensure to generate unique id for twice times for the same period.
the seed must be at least 1 to 2\*\*13 range. if we want to ensure uniqueness for two hours (100% contingency), we need
a seed for 1 to 2\*\*14 range. that's what we have with this algorithm. You have to increment seed_range_bits if you
move your machine by airplane to another time zone or if you have a glucky wallet and use a computer that can generate
more than 1 million ids per second.

one word about predictability : This algorithm is absolutely NOT designed to generate unpredictable unique id.
you can add a sha-1 or sha-256 digest step at the end of this algorithm but you will loose uniqueness and enter to collision probability world.
hash algorithms ensure that for same id generated here, you will have the same hash but for two differents id (a pair of ids), it is
possible to have the same hash with a very little probability. You would certainly take an option on a bijective function that maps
35 digits (or more) number to 35 digits (or more) number based on cipher block and secret key. read paper on breaking PRNG algorithms
in order to be convinced that problems could occur as soon as you use random library :)

1 million id per second ?... on a Intel(R) Core(TM)2 CPU 6400 @ 2.13GHz, you get :

```pycon
>>> timeit.timeit(uniqueid,number=40000)
1.0114529132843018
```

an average of 40000 id/second

modules.name: uidom.alpinejs.dataset
documentation:
This module contains data_* event functions that can be passed via 
DataState to its childrens.


eg: with DataState() as data_focused_pressed:
        data_focused()
        data_pressed()
    
    now use 
    with data_focusd_pressed:
        div("a"), div("b")
    
    both div's will receive attributes of data-focused and data-pressed.
Copyright (c) 2023 UiDOM
This software is released under the MIT License.
https://opensource.org/licenses/MIT

classes.name: uidom.alpinejs.dataset.MergeAlpineAttributes
documentation:
Merging the attributes with subcontexts via uidom.dom.src.dom_tag.attr

Args:
    None:
Usage:
    with MergeAlpineAttributes():
        attr(x_data=..., x_on_*=...)
        attr(x_data=..., x_on_*=...)
        div()
        ### this div receives all the attributes set via attr methods contexts
        ### but all the x_data, x_on_*... kwargs are merged..

classes.name: uidom.alpinejs.dataset.DataSet
